name: F-theory GUT + string axiverse + type-I seesaw (LVS moduli)
summary: Classification: KNOWN PROGRAM (top-down). The flagship top-down string-theoretic framework: F-theory compactified on an elliptic Calabi-Yau fourfold with 7-branes wrapping a del Pezzo surface produces an SU(5) (or SO(10)) GUT in 4D, with chiral matter from intersections and Yukawa hierarchies from local geometry. The Kahler moduli sector hosts the string axiverse — typically 10^2-10^3 ALPs spanning many decades in mass — of which one plays the role of the QCD axion solving strong CP. Large-Volume-Scenario stabilisation generates a hierarchy between the string scale and the GUT scale. Right-handed neutrinos in the 16 of SO(10) (or singlets in SU(5)) give the type-I seesaw. Distinguished from the SUSY-GUT framework by being a UV completion, not just a 4D Lagrangian: every parameter is in principle calculable from geometry. Distinguished from the bottom-up axiverse by deriving the axions from compactification rather than postulating them.

lineage.citation: Vafa, 'Evidence for F-theory', Nucl. Phys. B 469, 403 (1996)
Donagi and Wijnholt, 'Model Building with F-Theory', Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 15, 1237 (2011)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa, 'GUTs and Exceptional Branes in F-theory - I, II', JHEP 01 (2009) 058, JHEP 01 (2009) 059
Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell, 'String Axiverse', PRD 81, 123530 (2010)
Cicoli, Conlon, Quevedo, 'General Analysis of LARGE Volume Scenarios with String Loop Moduli Stabilisation', JHEP 0810, 105 (2008)
